extra.reasoning:
The property G(opted_out -> not sent) is VIOLATED via the t_resume_glitch transition.

Key observations:
1. t_resume_glitch fires on send_attempt when (opted_out AND stage >= 10), setting sent := true. Since stage is capped at 10, this means stage == 10. This sets sent true while opted_out is true — directly violating the property.
2. t_optout only sets opted_out := true and sent := false; it does NOT reset stage. So the lock stage persists across opt-out.
3. The advancing/reset transitions (adv_*, rst_*) are guarded only on stage, with no opted_out guard. So the lock can be dialed to stage 10 even while opted_out is true.

The advancing combination to reach stage 10: stage0->1 key_2, 1->2 key_1, 2->3 key_2, 3->4 key_1, 4->5 key_2, 5->6 key_0, 6->7 key_1, 7->8 key_1, 8->9 key_2, 9->10 key_1.

Concrete trace (opted_out, sent, stage):
init (F,F,0)
optout -> t_optout (T,F,0)
key_2 -> adv_0 (T,F,1)
key_1 -> adv_1 (T,F,2)
key_2 -> adv_2 (T,F,3)
key_1 -> adv_3 (T,F,4)
key_2 -> adv_4 (T,F,5)
key_0 -> adv_5 (T,F,6)
key_1 -> adv_6 (T,F,7)
key_1 -> adv_7 (T,F,8)
key_2 -> adv_8 (T,F,9)
key_1 -> adv_9 (T,F,10)
send_attempt -> t_resume_glitch (opted_out and stage>=10) (T,T,10)  <-- VIOLATION

Final state has opted_out=true and sent=true. Total events = 12, within the 13-event bound.
